2606.10906.title: Human-AI Teaming Through the Lens of Calibration
2606.10906.paper_summary: We study models for human-AI teaming through the lens of statistical calibration. We assume the team consists of an AI model and human -- both of which are calibrated with respect to some partitioning of the feature space -- and expose how the calibration assumptions propagate into the teaming framework. In particular, we consider frameworks that either (i) combine human and model predictions or (ii) delegate prediction responsibility to either a human or model. We show via theoretical and empirical results that existing methods for combination do not preserve the human's degree of calibration. Methods for delegation (by the very act of delegation) preserve calibration of the downstream predictors but shift the burden onto the rejector meta-model that decides who predicts. The rejector must be calibrated finely enough to locate where each member is superior, a demand that grows with the human's expertise and becomes unattainable when the human relies on information the system cannot observe.
2606.10906.paper_summary_zh: 我們通過統計校準的視角研究人類與AI團隊合作的模型。<lb/>我們假設團隊由一個AI模型和一個人類組成——這兩者都是相對於某種特徵空間的劃分進行校準的——並揭示校準假設如何傳遞到團隊框架中。<lb/>特別是，我們考慮的框架要麼是 (i) 結合人類和模型的預測，要麼是 (ii) 將預測責任委託給人類或模型。<lb/>我們通過理論和實證結果顯示，現有的組合方法並不保留人類的校準程度。<lb/>委託的方法（通過委託的行為）保留了下游預測者的校準，但將負擔轉移到決定誰進行預測的拒絕者元模型上。<lb/>拒絕者必須進行足夠精細的校準，以確定每個成員的優勢所在，這一要求隨著人類的專業知識增長而增加，並在當人類依賴系統無法觀察的信息時變得無法實現。
2606.10906.author: Eric Nalisnick et.al.
2606.10906.authors: Eric Nalisnick, Chi Zhang, Sophia Qian, Yixin Wang
